title: Blog Foundation — Basic Styles
description: Every great blog theme starts with solid foundation styles. In this first step, set base styles for the body element (font family, size, line height, text color, and background color), then style anchor links and the main heading (h1). Use a sans-serif font stack so the blog looks clean on any system.
test_code:
assert 'body' in css.lower(), 'Missing body selector'
assert 'font-family' in css.lower(), 'Missing font-family property'
assert 'sans-serif' in css.lower(), 'Font stack should include sans-serif as a fallback'
assert 'color' in css.lower(), 'Missing color property'
assert 'background-color' in css.lower(), 'Missing background-color property'
assert 'line-height' in css.lower(), 'Missing line-height property'
assert css.lower().count('a') >= 1 and ('a {' in css.lower() or 'a{' in css.lower()), 'Missing a (anchor) selector'
assert 'h1' in css.lower(), 'Missing h1 selector'
acceptance_criteria: body rule sets font-family with a sans-serif fallback stack
body has font-size, line-height, color, and background-color
hints: A common sans-serif stack: font-family: 'Segoe UI', Tahoma, Geneva, Verdana, sans-serif;
A comfortable line-height for reading is between 1.5 and 1.8
Use a dark color like #333 for text and a light background like #f9f9f9
